methods.description: This method measures the cytosolic pH in diatoms using the ratiometric H+-indicator BCECF. It involves in vitro and in situ calibration of the indicator to ensure accurate pH measurements.

steps.description: In vitro calibration of BCECF-free acid was performed by loading 5 mM BCECF in a buffer containing approximate cytosolic concentrations of major ions.
steps.searchable_summary: perform in vitro calibration of BCECF with ion buffer

steps.description: The ratio of fluorescence emission at 535-630 nm was measured with sequential excitation at λ1 = 485-625 nm and λ2 = 436-610 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: measure fluorescence emission ratio at specified wavelengths

steps.description: Images were acquired using a 100x PL APO 100x/1.40-0.70 Oil CS objective on a DM IRB microscope coupled to a Cool Snap HQ CCD camera.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire images using specified microscope and camera

steps.description: In situ calibration was performed to confirm that the cytosolic pH indicator responded to changes in intracellular pH in the same manner as for in vitro calibration.
steps.searchable_summary: perform in situ calibration of pH indicator

steps.description: Different rows of cells were treated at external pH values ranging from 6.4 to 8.5, and background fluorescence was determined in a row of unloaded cells.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze fluorescence at different external pH values

steps.description: To measure intracellular pH values, cells were first loaded with 10-12 mM BCECF-AM for 20 min, resulting in a final cytosolic concentration that yielded similar fluorescence intensity to the intensity obtained with the dye alone.
steps.searchable_summary: load cells with BCECF-AM and measure fluorescence

methods.description: This method measures the kinetics of frustule formation in diatoms using the fluorescent dye HCK-123. It involves real-time tracking of dye incorporation and fluorescence quantification.

steps.description: Exponentially growing T. weissflogii cells were introduced into a simple micro-fluidic device, with controlled temperature and light intensity.
steps.searchable_summary: introduce cells into microfluidic device with controlled conditions

steps.description: To record the time course of HCK-123 incorporation into individual cells, specific shell-scripts and C-modules were developed for cell tracking and fluorescence quantification.
steps.searchable_summary: develop scripts for cell tracking and fluorescence quantification

steps.description: Quantitatively evaluate the evolution of HCK-123 concentrations using a serial dilution and calibration curve.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate HCK-123 concentration using calibration curve

steps.description: Calculate the overall duration of the two early periods (t Exp and t Dec) from 6 independent experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate duration of valve formation periods
outputs: duration of t Exp and t Dec

methods.method_name: Microfluidics Experiment Setup, Data Acquisition and Analyses
methods.description: This method describes the setup of a microfluidic experiment for observing diatom cells, including data acquisition and analysis using fluorescence imaging.

steps.description: Cells were concentrated by gentle centrifugation and re-suspended in a fresh medium containing 1 mM HCK-123.
steps.searchable_summary: concentrate cells and resuspend in medium with HCK-123

steps.description: Cells were introduced into a capillary device connected to syringe pumps for medium renewal.
steps.searchable_summary: introduce cells into capillary device with syringe pump

steps.description: Cells were imaged on a single plane every 5 to 7 min using a specific objective.
steps.searchable_summary: image cells at regular intervals using microscope

steps.description: Image analyses were performed using Metamorph software and ImageJ.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze images using Metamorph and ImageJ
